rule: Workflow structure is formal and queryable; component-step internals stay flexible unless a stronger contract is explicitly needed.

attachment_points: startup, config, planning, memory, or adapter-surface scope tags

fields.purpose: bounded repo-local expectation worth surfacing into active work
lifecycle position where the component applies
module, surface, or work-scope tags that activate the component
bounded proof, refresh, or inspection commands expected at that stage
compact reminder for review, handoff, or closure surfaces

composition.selector: stage plus intersection of scope_tags with current work tags
composition.conflict_rule: Do not merge ownership; workspace reports applicable components and the owning module keeps domain behavior.
composition.module_attachment_rule: Components may mention planning, memory, workspace, startup, routing, adapter-surfaces, or system-intent tags, but they must not move module-owned state into workspace config.

leave_flexible: local implementation steps inside the bounded component
task-specific validation beyond declared minimum hooks
agent judgment about whether the component is sufficient for the current slice
must_not: turn every repo preference into a durable component
move planning or memory domain ownership into workspace config

migration_posture.markdown_role: Markdown workflow files remain adapters and explanatory runbooks when the contract is not yet expressible in the formal component shape.
migration_posture.promotion_rule: Promote repeated, stable, stage-bound workflow expectations into workflow components when they need selective query, reporting, or review hooks.
migration_posture.compatibility_rule: Keep prose fallback for nuance, but prefer the formal component for startup, report, and defaults answers.
